mission: Convert 35 plugin-style tools from .opencode/tool/ into built-in Effect tools in packages/opencode/src/tool/, integrated with DuckDB analytical infrastructure.

architecture.write_path: pipeline.ts spawns duckdb WITHOUT -readonly — the only write-capable path. Creates _pipeline_meta, _pipeline_session, _pipeline_part tables + analytics views.
architecture.read_path: db.duckdb.ts DuckDB.Service spawns WITH -readonly — agent-facing read-only client. Connection pool (max 4). CLI subprocess via duckdb binary.
architecture.firewall: duckdb-firewall.ts blocks read_json_auto, read_parquet, read_csv, ATTACH, LOAD, EXPORT for agent-facing queries.
architecture.config: duckdb-config.ts reads OPENCODE_DUCKDB_PATH env var (optional). When unset, DuckDB is unavailable — tools work via JSONL fallback.
architecture.pipeline_sequence: metaCheck → export sessions → export parts → CREATE_META_TABLE → CREATE_SESSION_TABLE → CREATE_PART_TABLE → createTables (NEW) → initViewsSql → WRITE_META

known_limitations: DuckDB.Service is READ-ONLY — tools that write must use separate non-readonly subprocess
No write pool service exists yet — tools need write helper (execDuckDBWrite)

plugin_tool_patterns.common_structure: import { tool } from "@opencode-ai/plugin" / import { spawnSync } from "node:child_process" / function runPython() / export default tool({ description, args, async execute })
plugin_tool_patterns.python_script_pattern: Each tool embeds a Python script as String.raw literal in the execute function. Python handles file I/O, JSON parsing, dedup, date arithmetic via spawnSync('python3', ['-', ...args])
plugin_tool_patterns.runtime_context: context.worktree (base path), context.sessionID, context.agent — all available in Tool.Context as ctx.sessionID, ctx.agent, InstanceState.context.directory

special_spawn: rig_jsonl_query.ts — spawns uv run --with duckdb python3 -

builtin_tool_pattern_tool_define.pattern: Tool.define("name", Effect.gen(function* () { const deps = yield* ...; return { description, parameters: Schema.Struct({...}), execute: (params) => Effect.gen(function* () { ... }).pipe(Effect.orDie) } }))
builtin_tool_pattern_tool_define.context_type: Tool.Context { sessionID, messageID, agent, abort, messages, ask, metadata }
builtin_tool_pattern_tool_define.description_source: Some tools use DESCRIPTION from <name>.txt files (send-message.txt, read-messages.txt, etc.)
builtin_tool_pattern_tool_define.init_pattern: Tool.init(tool) wraps the effect-produced definition into a Tool.Def with id, parameters, jsonSchema, execute
builtin_tool_pattern_tool_define.export_pattern: export * as Name from "./name" — namespace re-export per Module shape convention

duckdb_aware_dual_backend.duckdb_table: reads JSONL files via DuckDB subprocess

dirty_preserved_patterns: Lines 261-266 have indentation inconsistencies (tab stop vs spaces for coordination tool block)
registry_state.layer_provisioning: DuckDBConfig.defaultLayer is already provisioned (line 434). DuckDB.layer is provisioned (line 437). No new services needed.
registry_state.registration_pattern: yield* XTool → Tool.init(xTool) in Effect.all → tool.x_tool → entry in builtin array

conversion_approach.method: Each .opencode/tool/<name>.ts file gets a corresponding packages/opencode/src/tool/<name>.ts (using kebab-case) using Tool.define + Effect pattern. Python logic (file I/O, JSON parsing, dedup, date handling) gets replaced with direct TypeScript/Effect equivalents.
conversion_approach.file_naming: kebab-case.ts for built-in (propose-plan.ts), underscore for plugin (propose_plan.ts)
conversion_approach.tool_naming: snake_case in Tool.define("tool_name"), PascalCase for namespace export (export * as ToolName)
conversion_approach.dual_backend_approach: Always write to JSONL first (primary persistence). Optionally write to DuckDB via execDuckDBWrite helper when DuckDBConfig signals availability.
conversion_approach.dedup_strategy: For tools with dedup (tool_feedback, out_of_scope_finding, publish_finding): read existing JSONL, parse lines, check dedup_key (SHA-256 hash). TypeScript has crypto.subtle for SHA-256.
conversion_approach.timestamp_approach: Use Date.now() for Unix epoch millis or new Date().toISOString() for ISO 8601. Effect.DateTime.nowAsDate also available.

risk_python_to_ts_translation.detail: Plugin tools embed Python scripts (50-185 lines each) for file I/O, JSON parsing, dedup, SHA-256, subprocess. Must be replaced with TypeScript equivalents. Most complex: read_source.ts (~185 lines Python), smart_edit.ts (~90 lines), out_of_scope_finding.ts (~103 lines).
risk_python_to_ts_translation.mitigation: Start with simplest tools first (tool-failure, QA, wave records) to establish pattern, then tackle complex tools.

risk_duckdb_write_contention.detail: DuckDB supports 1 writer + multiple readers. Multiple tools writing concurrently could cause lock contention.
risk_duckdb_write_contention.mitigation: File writes as JSONL primary path. DuckDB writes are optional enhancement. Use execDuckDBWrite helper pattern.

risk_registry_dirty_state.detail: registry.ts has pre-existing dirty modifications from other lanes. Additive edits required—no reformatting or removing existing entries.
risk_registry_dirty_state.mitigation: Add new imports in alphabetical order in import block, add yield* init calls before the Effect.all block, add Tool.init entries and builtin array items at the end.

risk_tool_count.detail: 27 new tools to create + 6 to merge = 33 files to touch. 70+ registrations to add to registry.ts. High volume increases risk of errors.
risk_tool_count.mitigation: Batch into groups of 2-4 tools per delegation. Verify each batch with typecheck.

current_execution_state.phase_1: COMPLETE — schema tables added, pipeline.ts updated
current_execution_state.phase_3_5: NOT_STARTED — registry update, typecheck, test, final checkpoint
